issues.title: [Epic · Post-MVP] Runtime, analytics, replay, and platform expansion
issues.outcome: Complete the runtime, analytics, replay, and platform expansion outcome without pulling later platform work into the current gate.
scope: Close the phase only with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every child issue has a clear owner, milestone, and acceptance criteria.
Blocked work is represented using issue dependencies.
The phase gate has explicit evidence.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Runtime] Reconnect product pages to Rust/WASM viewer contracts after wiki release
issues.outcome: Reassess public runtime integration only after the wiki MVP is stable and a new product/legal decision approves it.
scope: Define a separate viewer product flow and bundle boundary.
Re-evaluate data, privacy, performance, and maintenance costs.
acceptanceCriteria: No runtime integration blocks Web MVP 0.1.
A later ADR defines public value and legal inputs.
Runtime remains testable independently.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Runtime] Expand legal AFF fixture and parser regression corpus
issues.outcome: Expand runtime test data when chart viewer work resumes.
scope: Legal fixtures, malformed examples, and documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture work is isolated from public wiki content.
No protected chart is committed.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Runtime] AFF support matrix and parser coverage roadmap
issues.outcome: Resume parser coverage decisions only when a public runtime milestone is approved.
scope: Support matrix, validation behavior, and legal test inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: No parser expansion blocks the wiki MVP.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Runtime] Timing and renderer correctness expansion
issues.outcome: Expand deterministic runtime tests after viewer scope is approved.
scope: Timing, snapshots, renderer, SVG, and performance tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Current tests remain green; expansion is outside Web MVP 0.1.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Analytics] Deterministic chart metrics and player-facing analysis
issues.outcome: Design chart analytics after the wiki product and legal chart-input path are stable.
scope: Metric definitions, explainability, contracts, and UI.
acceptanceCriteria: No analytics placeholder is shown in MVP.
Later work uses deterministic evidence and avoids unsupported difficulty claims.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Runtime] Typed WASM contract hardening for public viewer surfaces
issues.outcome: Harden runtime contracts only when public UI integration resumes.
scope: Versioning, generated types, compatibility tests, and error states.
acceptanceCriteria: Preserved runtime remains independently testable.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Data] Community-scale dataset publishing and provenance operations
issues.outcome: Scale beyond the hand-reviewed MVP catalog after contribution volume and update frequency justify it.
scope: Review queues, snapshot publication, provenance operations, and governance.
acceptanceCriteria: Static MVP workflow is measured before adding complexity.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Platform] Optional hosted API, search, and persistence decision
issues.outcome: Add hosted services only when measured static-site limits require them.
scope: Decision triggers, cost, operations, privacy, migration, and rollback.
acceptanceCriteria: No backend is implemented speculatively.
GitHub Student options are evaluated against real requirements.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Post-MVP Product] Local-first replay/import privacy and analysis roadmap
issues.outcome: Keep replay and personal analysis local-first and privacy-reviewed when the feature is reconsidered.
scope: Consent, supported inputs, browser storage, deletion, and runtime integration.
acceptanceCriteria: No replay data is collected by the wiki MVP.
A legal supported-input contract and privacy threat model are approved before implementation.
Parsing and storage require explicit user action; no replay or personal-analysis data is uploaded by default.
Users can inspect, export where supported, and delete all locally stored replay or analysis data.
Malformed files, oversized inputs, storage-quota failures, and schema migrations fail without corrupting an existing valid local dataset.
Hosted synchronization, telemetry, or cross-device persistence requires a separate security, privacy, cost, and operations decision.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.
